 {"id":"bd-3954","title":"Task: Create FileReservationWatcher module","description":"## Purpose
Create a background watcher that monitors pane output and auto-reserves files.

## Module Location
internal/watcher/file_reservation.go

## FileReservationWatcher Struct
type FileReservationWatcher struct {
    client             *agentmail.Client
    projectDir         string
    activeReservations map[string]time.Time  // pane -> last activity
    mu                 sync.Mutex
}

## Key Methods
func (w *FileReservationWatcher) Start(ctx context.Context)
func (w *FileReservationWatcher) checkPaneOutputs()
func (w *FileReservationWatcher) OnFileEdit(pane Pane, files []string)
func (w *FileReservationWatcher) releaseIdleReservations()

## Watcher Loop
- Poll every 10 seconds
- For each pane, capture recent output
- Parse for file edit patterns
- Reserve new files detected
- Release reservations for idle panes (10m+ inactive)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Background watcher starts with session
- Detects file edits from pane output
- Reserves files automatically in Agent Mail
- Releases reservations on idle
- Thread-safe operation

## Technical Notes
Use goroutine with context for lifecycle
Coordinate with session lifecycle events","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:41:19.522299399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T16:11:37.901182127Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T16:11:37.901094762Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3954","depends_on_id":"bd-dz3r","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:58:25Z","created_by":"import"}]}

Create an interactive setup wizard for non-technical users using Bubble Tea TUI framework.

## Wizard Flow
1. Project type selection (Go/Python/Node/Rust/Other)
2. Agent count configuration
3. Integration toggles (Agent Mail, CASS, CM)
4. File reservation auto-enable toggle
5. Confirmation and summary

## UI Components
- Selection list for project type
- Number input for agent count
- Toggle/checkbox for integrations
- Progress indicator during setup
- Summary display at end

## Acceptance Criteria
- Wizard runs by default, --non-interactive skips it
- All options configurable through wizard
- Escape key exits cleanly
- Summary shows all chosen options before confirming

## Technical Notes
Use existing Bubble Tea patterns from internal/tui/
Match dashboard styling (Catppuccin theme)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:31:55.603636143Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T09:07:55.578235604Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T09:07:55.578151726Z","close_reason":"Interactive Bubble Tea wizard already implemented in init_wizard.go","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-39uo","depends_on_id":"bd-38gl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:58:25Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3acv","title":"Task: Create Archiver background service","description":"## Purpose
Implement background archiver that captures agent output for CASS indexing.

## Archiver Struct
type Archiver struct {
    sessionName string
    outputDir   string
    interval    time.Duration
    lastOffsets map[string]int // pane -> last archived line
}

## Archive Loop
func (a *Archiver) Run(ctx context.Context) {
    ticker := time.NewTicker(a.interval)
    for {
        select {
        case <-ctx.Done():
            a.flush() // Final archive on shutdown
            return
        case <-ticker.C:
            a.archiveNewContent()
        }
    }
}

## Output Format (CASS-compatible JSONL)
{"session": "myproject", "pane": "cc_1", "agent": "claude", "timestamp": "2026-01-19T10:30:00Z", "content": "..."}

## Acceptance Criteria
- Background archiving with configurable interval
- Incremental capture (no duplicates)
- Flush on shutdown
- CASS-compatible output format

## Technical Notes
Use goroutine with context

- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-3ah0k","title":"Implement interrupt_test probe method","description":"Implement interrupt_test probe method.

## Method

Send Ctrl-C and observe response. More aggressive but definitive.

## When to Use

- When keystroke_echo is ambiguous
- With --aggressive flag
- As confirmation before kill

## Implementation

```go
func probeInterruptTest(session string, pane int, timeout time.Duration) ProbeResult {
    baseline := capturePaneContent(session, pane)
    
    // Send single Ctrl-C (not the double-tap that exits)
    exec.Command("/usr/bin/tmux", "send-keys", "-t", target, "C-c").Run()
    
    // Wait for response
    deadline := time.Now().Add(timeout)
    for time.Now().Before(deadline) {
        current := capturePaneContent(session, pane)
        if current != baseline {
            // Check if it's showing interrupt response
            return ProbeResult{
                Responsive: true,
                Method: "interrupt_test",
            }
        }
        time.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)
    }
    
    return ProbeResult{Responsive: false}
}
```

## Warning

This method may:
- Interrupt ongoing work
- Cause loss of in-progress output
- Not be recoverable

Document clearly in output that this is disruptive.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:33:10.174682714Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T18:47:36.046096410Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T18:47:36.046033962Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ah0k","depends_on_id":"bd-1cu1f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:33:10.203540339Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ah0k","depends_on_id":"bd-58w0z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:44:55.243574780Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ah0k","depends_on_id":"bd-ok7rj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:44:47.782662865Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-ptu2","title":"Task: Add event markers and details overlay","description":"## Overview
Add discrete event markers on the timeline with hover/select details.

## Deliverables
1. Event marker types:
   - Prompt sent (▶): When user sends prompt to agent
   - Completion (✓): When agent finishes a task
   - Error (✗): When error detected
   - Start/Stop (◆): Session lifecycle events

2. Marker rendering:
   - Small icons above timeline bar at event time
   - Different colors for different event types
   - Clustered markers when events too close together

3. Details overlay:
   - Show popup when marker selected
   - Include: timestamp, event type, details
   - For prompts: first 50 chars of message
   - For errors: error message

4. Interaction:
   - Arrow keys to navigate between markers
   - Enter to expand marker details
   - Esc to close overlay

## Implementation Notes
- Use unicode symbols for markers
- Popup positioning to avoid edge overflow
- Cache marker positions for efficient navigation

## Acceptance Criteria
- Markers appear at correct times
- Different event types visually distinct
- Details overlay shows on selection
- Navigation between markers works
- Unit tests for marker placement","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:28:03.496917374Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T18:28:50.481943421Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ptu2","depends_on_id":"bd-3h0n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:28:50.481921380Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ptu2","depends_on_id":"bd-f815","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:28:03.512975659Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-qjsu","title":"E2E Tests: File reservation with real Agent Mail","description":"## E2E Test: ntm + Agent Mail File Reservation Integration

**Note:** File reservation is implemented in MCP Agent Mail. This tests ntm's integration with that existing system.

### Prerequisites
- MCP Agent Mail server running (`am` health check passes)
- Test workspace with beads

### Test Scenarios

1. **Basic reservation flow**
   - Spawn agent via ntm
   - Assign bead with file paths
   - Verify Agent Mail shows reservation for agent
   - Complete bead
   - Verify reservation released

2. **Conflict detection**
   - Spawn 2 agents
   - Agent 1 assigned bead touching foo.go
   - Agent 2 assigned bead touching foo.go
   - Verify ntm reports conflict from Agent Mail
   - Verify holder info shown

3. **Reservation on assign**
   - Run ntm assign with --auto
   - Verify file_reservation_paths called for each assignment
   - Check Agent Mail state reflects reservations

### Verification Commands
```bash
# Check Agent Mail for reservations
# (use Agent Mail MCP tools or am CLI)
```

### Logging
- Log Agent Mail API calls
- Log reservation state at each step
- Log conflict details

## Test File
tests/e2e/test_file_reservation_integration.sh","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:14:22.491105889Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T02:46:46.058716925Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T02:46:46.058672331Z","close_reason":"Added e2e Agent Mail reservation tests; go test blocked by no space left on device","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qjsu","depends_on_id":"bd-dz3r","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:14:22.511980074Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-qjsu","depends_on_id":"bd-r5vz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:19:51.099332584Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"bd-r6d5e","title":"E2E Tests: ntm spawn --assign full workflow","description":"## Test Coverage
Full end-to-end test of ntm spawn --assign convenience flag.

## Test Scenario
1. Create test workspace with beads
2. Run ntm spawn --assign (single command)
3. Verify agents spawned
4. Verify beads assigned
5. Verify prompts received by agents
6. Verify combined output

## Test Script (tests/e2e/test_spawn_assign.sh)
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

LOG=/tmp/ntm-e2e-spawn-assign-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log
exec > >(tee -a $LOG) 2>&1

echo "[INFO] Starting ntm spawn --assign E2E test at $(date)"

# Setup
echo "[STEP 1] Creating test workspace..."
TEST_DIR=/tmp/ntm-spawn-assign-test-$$
mkdir -p $TEST_DIR
cd $TEST_DIR
br init
for i in {1..4}; do br create "Test bead $i" --type task; done
echo "[OK] Created 4 test beads"

# Spawn + Assign in one command
echo "[STEP 2] Running ntm spawn --assign..."
RESULT=$(ntm spawn ntm-spawn-assign-$$ --cc=2 --no-user --assign --strategy=round-robin --json)
echo "[DEBUG] Result: $RESULT"

# Verify spawn
echo "[STEP 3] Verifying spawn..."
AGENT_COUNT=$(echo $RESULT | jq '.spawn.agents | length')
if [ "$AGENT_COUNT" -ne 2 ]; then
    echo "[FAIL] Expected 2 agents, got $AGENT_COUNT"
    exit 1
fi
echo "[OK] 2 agents spawned"

# Verify assign
echo "[STEP 4] Verifying assignments..."
ASSIGNED=$(echo $RESULT | jq '.assign.assigned | length')
if [ "$ASSIGNED" -lt 2 ]; then
    echo "[FAIL] Expected at least 2 assignments, got $ASSIGNED"
    exit 1
fi
echo "[OK] $ASSIGNED beads assigned"

# Cleanup
echo "[STEP 5] Cleaning up..."
ntm kill ntm-spawn-assign-$$
rm -rf $TEST_DIR
echo "[OK] Cleanup complete"

echo "[PASS] ntm spawn --assign E2E test passed at $(date)"
```

## Logging Requirements
- Timestamp each step
- Log combined output
- Log spawn and assign phases separately
- Save full log to /tmp

 {"id":"bd-r85h","title":"E2E Tests: Staggered spawn and prompt delivery","description":"## End-to-End Test Suite for Smart Staggering

### E2E Test Scenarios
1. **Basic staggered spawn**
   - Spawn 5 agents with --stagger=5s
   - Verify ~5s between each spawn
   - Verify all eventually running

2. **Staggered prompt delivery**
   - Spawn session
   - Send prompt to all with stagger
   - Verify delivery times
   - Verify order preserved

3. **Adaptive staggering**
   - Enable rate limit detection
   - Trigger rate limit
   - Verify stagger increases
   - Verify recovers when clear

4. **Dependency-aware stagger**
   - Tasks with deps
   - Staggered assignment
   - Verify blockers complete before blocked

### Detailed Logging

log.Printf("[E2E-STAGGER] Interval: %v (base=%v, adaptive=%v)", actual, base, adjustment)
log.Printf("[E2E-STAGGER] Rate limit detected, increasing delay to %v", newDelay)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:15:51.751209452Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T06:20:25.568094549Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-r85h","depends_on_id":"bd-1g3f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:20:25.568070564Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-r85h","depends_on_id":"bd-ayjn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:15:51.774284256Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-r9fy","title":"Implement global animation tick source for synchronized animations","description":"## Problem
Multiple animated components with independent tick counters can cause visual noise.

## Code Review Findings (2026-01-19)

### Good News: Dashboard Already Uses Global Tick

**Central tick source exists:**
- `dashboard.go:42-43` - `DashboardTickMsg` message type  
- `dashboard.go:224` - `animTick int` in Model
- `dashboard.go:745` - Single 100ms tick: `tea.Tick(time.Millisecond*100, ...)`
- `dashboard.go:1599-1600` - Handler increments `m.animTick++`

**All animations use the global tick:**
```go
// dashboard.go:2618 - progress bar uses m.animTick
barContent := styles.ShimmerProgressBar(percent/100.0, barWidth, "█", "░", m.animTick, colors...)

// dashboard.go:2142 - ticker panel receives animTick
m.tickerPanel.SetAnimTick(m.animTick)

// layout.go:238 - layout functions receive tick parameter
bar := styles.ShimmerProgressBar(percent/100, width-4, "█", "░", tick, ...)

// dashboard.go:2718 - spinner uses animTick
statusIcon = WorkingSpinnerFrame(m.animTick)
```

### Orphaned Code in components/progress.go

The `components/progress.go` file has UNUSED independent tick mechanisms:
- Line 132: 50ms tick for ProgressBar
- Line 166: 50ms tick for IndeterminateBar
- Line 224: 50ms tick for IndeterminateBar.Init()

**These are NOT used by the dashboard.** The dashboard uses `styles.ShimmerProgressBar()` directly, which takes a tick parameter.

## Updated Fix Approach

### 1. Clean Up Orphaned Code
```go
// components/progress.go - Consider deprecating or removing the independent tick
// since dashboard uses styles.ShimmerProgressBar() with global tick instead

// Option A: Deprecate
// Deprecated: Use styles.ShimmerProgressBar() with global tick instead
func (p ProgressBar) Init() tea.Cmd { ... }

// Option B: Remove entirely if unused
```

### 2. Document Global Tick Pattern
Add to CONTRIBUTING.md:
```markdown
## Animation Synchronization

All animations in the dashboard use a global tick source:

1. The dashboard maintains `animTick int` incremented every 100ms
2. Pass `animTick` to all animated rendering functions
3. Never create independent tea.Tick sources in subcomponents
4. Use `styles.Shimmer()`, `styles.ShimmerProgressBar()` with tick parameter

Example:
\`\`\`go
// Good: use global tick
bar := styles.ShimmerProgressBar(percent, width, "█", "░", m.animTick, colors...)

// Bad: independent tick in subcomponent
func (m MyModel) Init() tea.Cmd {
    return tea.Tick(time.Millisecond*50, ...) // Creates separate timer
}
\`\`\`
```

### 3. Add Test for Tick Synchronization
```go
func TestAllAnimationsUseGlobalTick(t *testing.T) {
    // Verify that styles.Shimmer produces deterministic output for given tick
    result1 := styles.Shimmer("test", 42, "#FF0000", "#00FF00")
    result2 := styles.Shimmer("test", 42, "#FF0000", "#00FF00")
    
    if result1 != result2 {
        t.Error("Shimmer should be deterministic for same tick value")
    }
    
    // Different ticks produce different results
    result3 := styles.Shimmer("test", 43, "#FF0000", "#00FF00")
    if result1 == result3 {
        t.Error("Different ticks should produce different shimmer effects")
    }
}
```

## Verification Checklist
- [x] Dashboard uses single DashboardTickMsg source (100ms)
- [x] animTick passed to all animated components  
- [ ] Remove/deprecate orphaned tick code in components/progress.go
- [ ] Document global tick pattern in CONTRIBUTING.md
- [ ] Verify NTM_REDUCE_MOTION=1 disables all animation

 {"id":"bd-rjlw","title":"Error Handling Tests: Recovery failure scenarios","description":"## Error Handling Tests for Session Recovery

### Error Scenarios
1. **Tmux not running**
   - Test recovery when tmux server not started
   - Verify clear error message

2. **Corrupted state files**
   - Invalid JSON in state file
   - Missing required fields
   - State file permissions denied

3. **Pane recreation failures**
   - Target window doesn't exist
   - Shell command fails
   - Insufficient terminal size

4. **Agent Mail unavailable**
   - Recovery without Agent Mail MCP
   - File reservations cannot be restored

### Graceful Degradation
- Test recovery continues despite individual pane failures
- Test state file backup before overwrite
- Test rollback on catastrophic failure

### Logging

log.Printf("[RECOVERY-FALLBACK] Using degraded mode: %s", reason)

+# E2E Tests for Usage Limit Detection System
+# Bead: bd-1xn3e
+#
+# These tests verify the robot mode commands work correctly with real tmux sessions.
+# In CI, tests run in mock mode. For local testing with real agents, omit E2E_MOCK_MODE.
+#
+# Local usage with real agents:
+#   go test -v ./e2e/... -timeout 10m
+#
+# CI mock mode:
+#   E2E_MOCK_MODE=1 CAUT_MOCK_FILE=e2e/fixtures/caut_mock.json go test -v ./e2e/... -short
+
+name: E2E Tests
+
+on:
+  # Manual trigger for full E2E testing
+  workflow_dispatch:
+    inputs:
+      mock_mode:
+        description: 'Run in mock mode (no real agents)'
+        required: false
+        default: 'true'
+        type: boolean
+      test_filter:
+        description: 'Test filter pattern (e.g., TestE2E_Working)'
+        required: false
+        default: ''
+        type: string
+
+  # Run on PRs that touch e2e or robot code
+  pull_request:
+    branches: ["main"]
+    paths:
+      - 'e2e/**'
+      - 'internal/robot/**'
+      - 'internal/caut/**'
+      - '.github/workflows/e2e-tests.yml'
+
+  # Nightly scheduled run
+  schedule:
+    - cron: '0 4 * * *'  # 4 AM UTC daily
+
+concurrency:
+  group: e2e-${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
+  cancel-in-progress: true
+
+permissions:
+  contents: read
+
+env:
+  GO_VERSION: "1.25"
+
+jobs:
+  # ============================================================================
+  # E2E Tests - Mock Mode (CI)
+  # ============================================================================
+  e2e-mock:
+    name: E2E Tests (Mock Mode)
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    timeout-minutes: 15
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - name: Set up Go
+        uses: actions/setup-go@v5
+        with:
+          go-version: ${{ env.GO_VERSION }}
+          cache: true
+
+      - name: Install tmux
+        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y tmux
+
+      - name: Build ntm
+        run: |
+          go build -o ntm ./cmd/ntm
+          sudo mv ntm /usr/local/bin/
+
+      - name: Verify ntm installed
+        run: ntm version
+
+      - name: Run E2E Tests (Mock Mode)
+        env:
+          E2E_MOCK_MODE: "1"
+          CAUT_MOCK_FILE: "e2e/fixtures/caut_mock.json"
+          E2E_LOG_DIR: "/tmp/e2e-logs"
+        run: |
+          # Create log directory
+          mkdir -p "$E2E_LOG_DIR"
+
+          # Run tests with -short flag (skips long-running tests without real agents)
+          go test -v ./e2e/... -short -timeout 5m \
+            ${{ inputs.test_filter && format('-run {0}', inputs.test_filter) || '' }} \
+            2>&1 | tee e2e-output.txt
+
+      - name: Upload E2E Logs
+        if: always()
+        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
+        with:
+          name: e2e-logs-mock
+          path: |
+            /tmp/e2e-logs/
+            e2e-output.txt
+          retention-days: 7
+
+  # ============================================================================
+  # E2E Infrastructure Tests - Verify test suite compiles and runs basic checks
+  # ============================================================================
+  e2e-infra:
+    name: E2E Infrastructure Check
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    timeout-minutes: 10
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - name: Set up Go
+        uses: actions/setup-go@v5
+        with:
+          go-version: ${{ env.GO_VERSION }}
+          cache: true
+
+      - name: Install tmux
+        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y tmux
+
+      - name: Build and vet E2E tests
+        run: |
+          go build ./e2e/...
+          go vet ./e2e/...
+
+      - name: List E2E tests
+        run: go test ./e2e/... -list '.*' -count=1
+
+      - name: Run session-not-found test (no agents required)
+        run: |
+          go build -o ntm ./cmd/ntm
+          sudo mv ntm /usr/local/bin/
+          go test -v ./e2e/... -run TestE2E_SessionNotFoundError -timeout 2m
+
+  # ============================================================================
+  # E2E Robot Mode Validation
+  # ============================================================================
+  e2e-robot-mode:
+    name: Robot Mode Commands
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    timeout-minutes: 10
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - name: Set up Go
+        uses: actions/setup-go@v5
+        with:
+          go-version: ${{ env.GO_VERSION }}
+          cache: true
+
+      - name: Install tmux
+        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y tmux
+
+      - name: Build ntm
+        run: |
+          go build -o ntm ./cmd/ntm
+          sudo mv ntm /usr/local/bin/
+
+      - name: Create test tmux session
+        run: |
+          tmux new-session -d -s test_session -x 200 -y 50
+          # Give tmux time to initialize
+          sleep 2
+          tmux list-sessions
+
+      - name: Test --robot-is-working (empty session)
+        run: |
+          echo "Testing --robot-is-working on empty session..."
+          ntm --robot-is-working=test_session --panes=0 | jq .
+
+      - name: Test --robot-agent-health (empty session)
+        run: |
+          echo "Testing --robot-agent-health on empty session..."
+          ntm --robot-agent-health=test_session --panes=0 | jq .
+
+      - name: Test --robot-smart-restart dry-run
+        run: |
+          echo "Testing --robot-smart-restart with --dry-run..."
+          ntm --robot-smart-restart=test_session --panes=0 --dry-run | jq .
+
+      - name: Test error handling (non-existent session)
+        run: |
+          echo "Testing error handling for non-existent session..."
+          # Should return valid JSON with success=false
+          output=$(ntm --robot-is-working=nonexistent_session_xyz 2>&1 || true)
+          echo "$output" | jq -e '.success == false' || {
+            echo "Expected success=false for non-existent session"
+            echo "Output: $output"
+            exit 1
+          }
+
+      - name: Cleanup
+        if: always()
+        run: tmux kill-server 2>/dev/null || true
+
+  # ============================================================================
+  # Summary
+  # ============================================================================
+  e2e-success:
+    name: E2E Success
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    timeout-minutes: 5
+    needs: [e2e-mock, e2e-infra, e2e-robot-mode]
+    if: always()
+    steps:
+      - name: Check all E2E jobs passed
+        run: |
+          if [ "${{ needs.e2e-mock.result }}" != "success" ] || \
+             [ "${{ needs.e2e-infra.result }}" != "success" ] || \
+             [ "${{ needs.e2e-robot-mode.result }}" != "success" ]; then
+            echo "One or more E2E jobs failed"
+            exit 1
+          fi
+          echo "All E2E checks passed!"
